                          1970 Census data into the estimation procedures. For example, the civilian labor force and employment totals were increased
                          by «ore than 300,000 as a result of the census adjustment. For an explanation of the changes and an indication of the dif-
                          ferences, see "Revisions in the Current Population Survey" in the February 1972 issue ofEmployment and Earnings.

                     Data relate to employees in such occupations in the telephone industry as switchboard operators; service assistants; operating room instructors; and pay-station attendants. In 1971, such

                  t As noted on page 7 of the January 1972 issue of this magazine, the series does not include earned pay withheld as a consequence of delays caused by the wage freeze in effect between Au-
           gust 15 and November 14, 1971, and by administrative procedures of the Pay Board. These delays resulted from compliance with the Government's Economic Stabilization Program. Because these
           withheld payments were substantial and fell due under a significant number of new labor-management contracts in that industry, they affected a large proportion of SIC 372.-Accordingly, the series
           will be supplemented by publication in the future of a series which will include withheld payments in the months earned.

                                                Unemployment Insurance Data

               Insured unemployment represents the number of persons               as a percent of average covered employment in a 12-month
           reporting a week of unemployment under an unemployment in-              period ending 6 to 8 months prior to the week of reference.
           surance program. It includes some persons who are working part          Initial claims are notices filed by those losing jobs covered by an
           time who would be counted as employed in the payroll and                unemployment insurance program that they are starting a
           household surveys. Excluded are persons who have exhausted              period of unemployment. A claimant who continued to be
           their benefit rights and workers who have not earned rights to          unemployed a full week is then counted in the insured un-
           unemployment insurance. In general, excluded from coverage              employment figure.
           are those persons engaged in agriculture, domestic service, un-
           paid family work, selected nonprofit organizations, State and              Because of differences in State laws and procedures under
           local government and self-employment. Atso excluded from                which unemployment insurance programs are operated, State un-
           the insured unemployment count, but included as employed in             employment rates generally indicate, but do not precisely mea-
           the household survey, are those persons who earned no wages             ure, differences among the individual States. Persons wishing to
           during the payroll period because they were temporarily absent          receive a detailed description of the nature, sources, inclusions
           from their jobs due to taking time off, illness and industrial          and exclusions, and limitations of unemployment insurance data
